title: US Navy Achieves Triple-Carrier Presence in Middle East for First Time Since 2003 Iraq War
summary: The USS George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group joined the Abraham Lincoln CSG and Gerald R. Ford CSG in the US Central Command area, marking the first triple-carrier deployment in the region since 2003, supporting blockade operations outside the Gulf of Oman.
contents:
## Triple US Carrier Deployment in Middle East

### Force Posture Update
The US Navy has achieved triple-carrier coverage in the Middle East for the first time since the 2003 Iraq War. The George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group arrived in the US 5th Fleet area of responsibility, joining the Abraham Lincoln and Gerald R. Ford carrier strike groups.

### Deployed Forces
- **USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77)**: Operating in the Indian Ocean/Arabian Sea, recently arrived
- **USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72)**: Operating in the Arabian Sea, supporting blockade measures outside Gulf of Oman
- **USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78)**: On station in the northern Red Sea
- **USS Tripoli (LHA-7) ARG**: Also operating in the CENTCOM area

### Combined Capabilities
The three carrier strike groups represent approximately 14 acres of sovereign US territory, embarking 27 squadrons with more than 200 aircraft. Nine guided-missile destroyers carry 846 Vertical Launch System tubes loaded with offensive and defensive missiles. At least three fast-attack submarines provide additional undersea escort.

### Strategic Context
This massive naval concentration supports ongoing operations related to the Iran conflict, including the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and Iranian oil exports. The deployment demonstrates sustained US commitment to the region as ceasefire negotiations remain fragile.

### Rotation Planning
USS Theodore Roosevelt CSG continues workups in the US 3rd Fleet area, integrating with an Unmanned Surface Vessel Division operating medium USV Seahawk — a potential preview of future carrier strike group compositions.
